title: Chouchou Refuses to Abandon the Shop
description: On pages 17–20, Chouchou’s loyalty to the pet food shop turns Buggy’s occupation from a pirate threat into a personal wound for the town.
scene: Chouchou refuses to give up the place connected to his owner, while pirates and bystanders react to the dog’s stubborn defense.

dialogue.speaker: Nami
dialogue.text: NOW HAND OVER THE MAP OF THE GRAND LINE!!

dialogue.speaker: Luffy
dialogue.text: SO THAT'S WHAT YOU'RE AFTER. WELL, A COUPLE OF LILLY-LIVERED, NO-NAME PIRATES LIKE YOU WON'T LAST A DAY ON THE GRANDLINE!!

dialogue.speaker: Luffy
dialogue.text: WHAT DO YOU PLAN TO DO THERE!? GO SIGHT- SEEING!?

dialogue.speaker: Luffy
dialogue.text: I'm gonna be king of the pirates.

dialogue.speaker: Luffy
dialogue.text: If you're King of Pirates, then what am I!? God of the Pirates!?

dialogue.speaker: Nami
dialogue.text: The world's treasure will be mine! So forget it!

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #2
dialogue.text: Okay, hurry up and attack me.

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #1
dialogue.text: You'll soon regret your words, rubber boy!

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #1
dialogue.text: you and your straw hat remind me of MMM when he was younger...

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #1
dialogue.text: THAT INSOLENT DOG WITH THE RED HAIR!!!

summary: This part makes the conflict emotional: Luffy sees that Buggy’s violence is not abstract, but aimed at things people love and protect.
